title: Market Brief — Monday, June 29, 2026
summary: GPU compute is loosening fast. Util-avail stands at 32% for A100, 44% for H100, 49% for H200, and 35% for B200, placing every GPU class in comfortable mid-range territory with no scarcity signals. H100 posted the day's sharpest move, dropping 9% to $2.00/hr as utilization cratered 25.6 percentage points over 30 days, signaling demand destruction or workload migration to B200, the only class gaining utilization.
paragraphs: GPU compute is loosening fast. Util-avail stands at 32% for A100, 44% for H100, 49% for H200, and 35% for B200, placing every GPU class in comfortable mid-range territory with no scarcity signals. H100 posted the day's sharpest move, dropping 9% to $2.00/hr as utilization cratered 25.6 percentage points over 30 days, signaling demand destruction or workload migration to B200, the only class gaining utilization.

B200 is the only GPU where inference margins work at scale: TCO-adjusted cost of $4.23/M tokens on Llama 70B lets high-tier sellers pocket 44% gross margin. H200 operators selling at the medium tier face losses of $9.72/M tokens, a cash furnace. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output is aggressively below any on-prem TCO, burning cash to buy share. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

H100 spot at $2.00/hr with 55.6% utilization in freefall is overvalued relative to B200, which delivers 2x better inference economics at only 2.3x the hourly rate. Undervalued trade: lock B200 capacity near $4.63/hr before utilization, already climbing 8.6pp this month, tightens further.
bullets: 44% util-avail on H100 with a 25.6 percentage point 30-day utilization drop marks the fastest demand decline across all GPU classes.
35% util-avail on B200 is tightest among next-gen chips, consistent with its 8.6pp utilization gain over 30 days.
$80/M output for o3 Pro makes it 233x pricier than DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M, the widest spread in the 44-model stack.
127% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI is nearly 3x that of B200, making H200 the riskiest compute position to hold.
21.36 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token prices have fallen roughly 79% from the GPT-4 launch baseline of 100.
$3.38/M TCO-adjusted inference cost on B200 undercuts H100 by 42%, cementing Blackwell as the margin-positive architecture.
